name: content-signals-staff-annotations
commandName: content-signals.ambient (new ambient handler in botHandlers(), no slash command)
repositoryFileContent: See file written at packages/data/src/domain-reputation-repository.ts. Interface DomainReputationRepository { hasSeenDomain(tenantId, domain): Promise<boolean>; recordDomainSeen(tenantId, domain): Promise<void>; } plus PrismaDomainReputationRepository and InMemoryDomainReputationRepository, following the chat-setting-repository.ts pattern exactly. Since the backing Prisma model does not exist in schema.prisma yet (that file is off-limits to edit directly), the Prisma-backed class accesses `this.client` through a small local `DomainReputationClient` shape via a cast (`this.client as unknown as DomainReputationClient`) instead of the generated delegate type, so the file typechecks today; once the model below is added to schema.prisma and `prisma generate` runs, the same code calls the real generated delegate at runtime with no changes needed.
repositoryTestFileContent: 5 vitest cases against InMemoryDomainReputationRepository: unseen-until-recorded, normalization (case/www./trailing dot), per-tenant isolation, idempotency of recordDomainSeen, and empty/unparseable domain is a no-op. All 5 pass (see verification below).
prismaModelSnippet:
model DomainReputation {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId    String
  domain      String
  firstSeenAt DateTime @default(now())

  tenant Tenant @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([tenantId, domain])
  @@map("domain_reputation")
}
botUpdateServiceIntegrationNotes:
This wires the three PURE detector modules (pii-redaction.ts, qr-bait.ts, shortener-quarantine.ts) into the live per-message ambient pipeline for the first time — today they only exist as manual test commands (e.g. the existing `dominio_nuevo` case in the giant switch just calls detectNewDomain() directly on CLI-style args, it is not part of the real message flow). No existing case in handleUtilityPlusCommand needs to change; this is purely a new ambient handler, registered in botHandlers() like scam-signals.ambient / privacy-leak.ambient right above it.

Where things go in apps/bot/src/bot-update.service.ts (paste snippet pieces 1-6 above, in order):
1-3) Three import additions: DomainReputationRepository (type, from @superbot/data), InMemoryDomainReputationRepository (value, from @superbot/data), and buildShortenerSignal/detectPii/detectQrBait/hasDoxxingRisk (from @superbot/module-security, already the source of detectDoxxing/detectCamouflagedLink etc. used a few lines below in the same import block).
4) One new @Optional()-injected constructor parameter, domainReputationRepository, defaulting to InMemoryDomainReputationRepository() exactly like chatActivityRepository/chatSettingRepository above it — so nothing breaks in tests or anywhere DI doesn't provide the token.
5) One new entry in the botHandlers() array, 'content-signals.ambient', placed right after 'social-signals.ambient' (~line 1738) and before 'night-mode.ambient' — runs on every ordinary text message, same slot as the other two content-signal ambient checks.
6) Two new private methods placed right after handleScamSignalsAmbient() (~line 11162): handleContentSignalsAnnotation (the orchestrator) and the static extractMessageDomains helper (needed because no existing module extracts URL hostnames from raw text — camouflaged-link.ts only matches phrases, and packages/telegram/src/normalize.ts, which IS off-limits, only produces a boolean hasUrl flag, not a domain list).

Why nothing here is destructive: handleContentSignalsAnnotation NEVER deletes, mutes, warns, or replies — it always returns null (like handleChatActivityLogger). Its only side effect is `this.repository.recordAudit(...)`, the exact same audit-log write every other ambient handler in this file already performs (see e.g. the membershipgate.kick example at line ~3405), which is what makes the finding visible to STAFF (via whatever existing audit/case-note viewing surface already reads AuditLog — this task doesn't add a new viewer, it feeds the existing one). It also skips admin authors (same convention as handlePrivacyLeakAmbient/handleScamSignalsAmbient next to it) purely to reduce noise; that's a policy choice, not a safety gate, and can be dropped if STAFF wants admin messages flagged too.

Two shared files still need small additions this task deliberately does NOT make (per the 'don't touch shared in-flight files' constraint), since apps/bot/src/tokens.ts and apps/bot/src/app.module.ts both already show uncommitted changes from another concurrent process in git status:
- apps/bot/src/tokens.ts: add `export const DOMAIN_REPUTATION_REPOSITORY = Symbol("DOMAIN_REPUTATION_REPOSITORY");` next to CHAT_ACTIVITY_REPOSITORY/GUARDIAN_REPOSITORY.
- apps/bot/src/app.module.ts: import PrismaDomainReputationRepository and DOMAIN_REPUTATION_REPOSITORY from the same places CHAT_ACTIVITY_REPOSITORY/PrismaChatActivityRepository are imported from, then add `{ provide: DOMAIN_REPUTATION_REPOSITORY, useFactory: () => new PrismaDomainReputationRepository() }` to the providers array (right after the CHAT_ACTIVITY_REPOSITORY / GUARDIAN_REPOSITORY entries, ~line 314-322).
Until those two lines land, the constructor's default `new InMemoryDomainReputationRepository()` keeps the bot fully functional (in-memory, per-process, non-persistent seenBefore tracking) — so this can ship and be tested end-to-end on @Modryvatestbot before the DI wiring is added; only the 'seen before' memory won't survive a restart until the Prisma-backed provider is registered.
risks:
1) Prisma model not yet in schema.prisma: DomainReputationRepository's Prisma-backed class references a `DomainReputation` model that doesn't exist in packages/data/prisma/schema.prisma yet (that file is off-limits to me directly). I therefore had the Prisma-backed class talk to `this.client` through a small local structural type (cast), so packages/data/src/domain-reputation-repository.ts typechecks cleanly today (verified: `node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc -p packages/data/tsconfig.json --noEmit` produces zero errors referencing this file — the only errors in that run are pre-existing, from an unrelated untracked file packages/data/src/known-admin-repository.ts belonging to another concurrent process). Once prismaModelSnippet is merged into schema.prisma and `prisma generate` is re-run, the exact same code calls the real generated delegate at runtime with no further changes. Until then, only PrismaDomainReputationRepository is unusable at runtime (would throw, since the DB table doesn't exist) — InMemoryDomainReputationRepository (the constructor default) is fully usable and is what the bot will use until app.module.ts is wired.
2) DI wiring left undone on purpose: as explained in the integration notes, apps/bot/src/tokens.ts and apps/bot/src/app.module.ts both need one small addition each for the Prisma-backed repository to be used in production; I did not touch either file because both already show uncommitted changes from a concurrent process in `git status`, and touching them risked clobbering that other work. Net effect: the new detectors work today against an in-memory (per-process) domain-reputation store; persistence across restarts needs those two follow-up edits plus a migration.
3) Domain extraction is intentionally minimal: extractMessageDomains only matches http(s):// URLs via regex + `new URL().hostname` — it will not catch bare-text domains without a scheme (e.g. a message that just says 'bit.ly/x' with no protocol). This mirrors the existing codebase's content.hasUrl flag, which is also entity/scheme-oriented, so it's consistent with current detection fidelity, but a future improvement could widen the regex to bare-domain patterns if scam messages start omitting the scheme.
4) Admin exclusion is a judgment call, not a hard requirement from the task: I mirrored the two closest existing ambient handlers (handlePrivacyLeakAmbient, handleScamSignalsAmbient) which both skip admin authors, to keep this consistent and low-noise. If STAFF wants PII/QR-bait/shortener signals surfaced even for admin-authored messages, that one `isActorAdmin` guard should be removed — flagged explicitly in the integration notes and the method's own doc comment so it's easy to revisit.
5) Verification performed: `tsc --noEmit` on packages/data (clean for my file) and `vitest run packages/data/src/domain-reputation-repository.test.ts` (5/5 passing). I did NOT attempt to compile or run apps/bot/src/bot-update.service.ts with the snippet actually pasted in, since I was instructed not to edit that file — the snippet is Grep/Read-derived from the file's exact existing style (import blocks, constructor pattern, recordAudit shape, ambient-handler shape) but has not been literally typechecked in place.
